This find & replace updates the domain o: This find & replace updates the domain of all the matching URLs in your database. You may wonder why we have double slashes in front of the domain name. If we excluded those, it could replace your domain in unwanted places like email addresses. You probably don't want to replace joe@yourdomain.com with joe@staging.yourdomain.com. More details in our Find & Replace Doc →

<strong>Microsoft Edge Not Supported<\/s: Microsoft Edge Not Supported — Less than 2% of our customers use Microsoft Edge, so we've decided not to spend time supporting it. We ask that you use Firefox or a Webkit-based browser like Chrome or Safari instead. If this is a problem for you, please let us know.
Whoa! We've detected that the database t: Whoa! We've detected that the database table prefix in the import file does not match the database prefix of this install. Clicking the Import button below will create new database tables with the prefix "%s"

If you've found a bug, please <a href=\": If you've found a bug, please submit an issue at Github.
If you want a <strong>timely response vi: If you want a timely response via email from a developer who works on this plugin, upgrade to WP Migrate and send us an email.
You may ask the WordPress community for: You may ask the WordPress community for help by posting to the WordPress.org support forum. Response time can range from a few days to a few weeks and will likely be from a non-developer.
As this is a free plugin, we do not prov: As this is a free plugin, we do not provide support.
<strong>Internet Explorer Not Supported<: Internet Explorer Not Supported — Less than 2% of our customers use IE, so we've decided not to spend time supporting it. We ask that you use Firefox or a Webkit-based browser like Chrome or Safari instead. If this is a problem for you, please let us know.
Compatible with older versions of MySQL: Compatible with older versions of MySQL (pre-5.5)
Although the WordPress Codex emphasizes: Although the WordPress Codex emphasises that GUIDs should not be changed, this is limited to sites that are already live. If the site has never been live, we recommend replacing the GUIDs. For example, you may be developing a new site locally at dev.somedomain.com and want to migrate the site live to somedomain.com.
We couldn't connect over HTTPS but regul: We couldn't connect over HTTPS but regular HTTP appears to be working so we've switched to that. If you run a push or pull, your data will be transmitted unencrypted. Most people are fine with this, but just a heads up.
Connection Info - Site URL & Secret Key: Connection Info - Site URL & Secret Key
We've detected that your server supports:
We've detected that your server supports requests up to 25 MB, but
          it's possible that your server has limitations that we could not
          detect. To be on the safe side, we set the default to 1 MB, but you
          can try throttling it up to get better performance. If you're getting
          a 413 error or having trouble with time outs, try throttling this
          setting down.
As a result, uppercase characters in tab: As a result, uppercase characters in table names will be converted to lowercase during the migration.
